name: Tom Kha Kai
recipeIngredient: 16 fluid ounces soup broth (chicken stock) or water
2 kaffir lime leaves, rolled to crack them to release the flavor, but otherwise intact
2 inch piece lemon grass, bruised to release flavor
4 oz chicken breast cut into smallish bite sized pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the stock in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add the kaffir lime leaves, lemon grass, and galangal. Stir thoroughly.
3. Add fish sauce and lime juice, stirring well to combine.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil, then add the chicken pieces.
5. Reduce the heat to low and simmer until the chicken is cooked through (about 2 minutes).
6. Stir in the coconut milk and cook for an additional minute or two.
7. Taste and adjust seasoning with fish sauce and lime juice as needed.
8. Garnish with coriander leaves before serving.
Note: The number of red chilies is a personal choice, ranging from half a chili per diner to 8-10 per diner. For this recipe, use about 8-12 chilies. Serve with steamed white (jasmine) rice or as part of a Thai meal. This serves 4 people.
